heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: Stormy conditions are expected.  This is the first storm in the region since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 7th since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 9 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 23 December 2025.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 6 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (197°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 8.0°C), outside temperature (down 4.9°C), and feels like temperature (down 4.0°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 291 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 8.7°C), outside temperature (up 8.9°C), cloud levels (up 331 m), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 291.4m lower than yesterday, 331.0m lower than the past week, 27.2m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, cloud levels hit a low of 828m and a high of 2280m, averaging 989.5m; the past week and month reported somewhat higher averages.
cloud levels.summary: The sky may alternate between sunny and overcast.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; freezing temperatures are anticipated. Also, the atmosphere will feel parched and dry and winds will be practically absent. Chance of light rain, the sky may alternate between sunny and overcast, and bright intervals likely. High pressure will dominate, leading to stable weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-4.9°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
